title: Add --log-basic flag
body:
Signed-off-by: Ashley Cui <acui@redhat.com>

#### What type of PR is this?

/kind other

#### What this PR does / why we need it:
The log basic flag silences the pull progress messages. It's a medium between --quiet and regular logging behavior

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:
BZ 1694869

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes please follow the kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note

```

comments.body:
Overall a really nice fix @ashley-cui .  I thought this would be far more involved in the message suppression code, but it looks like most of the work was in the new option.

Just a couple tweaks to wording, then gtg!

Missing command completions.
Who asked for this?  Was this an issue assigned for this?

Since the redirected TTY takes care of this, I see no need for adding this complexity.  Closing.
Sorry @ashley-cui
Could we add a comment here and in the BZ on how to redirect the TTY for posterity's sake?

Simple bash redirection, IE Sending STDOUT to something other then the terminal, gets rid of the tty

buildah bud /tmp > /tmp/log
buildah bud /tmp | grep -i step
...

I believe even
buildah bud /tmp | cat

Should remove progress.
